'''Josaia Naulumatua "Joe" Lovodua''' (born 18 March 1998) is a Fijian professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays for [[Doncaster RLFC]] in the [[RFL Championship]]. He primarily plays as a utility. He has represented the [[Fiji national rugby league team|Fijian national team]], most notably at the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup]].

==Early life==
Lovodua was born in [[Lautoka]], [[Fiji]].

He played junior rugby league for [[Hurstville United]].

==Playing career==
===St. George Illawarra Dragons===
Lovodua began playing for the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]] in the [[2017 NRL Under-20s season|2017 Holden Cup]].<ref>{{Cite news |last=Narain |first=Pravin |url=http://www.fijitimes.com/story.aspx?id=419768 |title=Lovodua ready for Bati debut |date=2017-10-12 |work=Fiji Times |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171024043654/http://www.fijitimes.com/story.aspx?id=419768 |archive-date=2017-10-24}}</ref> In 2018 and 2019 he played in the [[NSW Cup|Canterbury Cup NSW]] for the Dragons.<ref name="nsw cup profile">{{Cite web |title=Joe Lovodua - NSW Cup profile |url=https://www.rabbitohs.com.au/teams/nsw-cup/south-sydney-rabbitohs-nsw/joe-lovodua/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210416235225/https://www.rabbitohs.com.au/teams/nsw-cup/south-sydney-rabbitohs-nsw/joe-lovodua/ |archive-date=2021-04-16 |access-date=2021-04-16 |website=[[South Sydney Rabbitohs]]}}</ref>
===South Sydney Rabbitohs===
In 2021, he joined [[South Sydney Rabbitohs|South Sydney]]'s [[NSW Cup]] team.<ref name="nsw cup profile" />
Lovodua joined [[Hull F.C.]] on a one-year contract for 2022.<ref>{{cite web|date=1 October 2021|title=Betfred Super League: Hull FC sign Fiji hooker Joe Lovodua on one-year deal for 2022 season|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12204/12423227/betfred-super-league-hull-fc-sign-fiji-hooker-joe-lovodua-on-one-year-deal-for-2022-season|work=Sky Sports|access-date=4 December 2021}}</ref>
===Hull FC===
He played 25 games with Hull F.C. in the 2022 Super League season as the club finished 9th. Lovodua played 19 matches for Hull F.C. for in the [[Super League XXVIII]] season as the club finished 10th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-fc-high-lows-mid-8799429/|title=Hull FC highs and lows as mid-season resurgence not enough to prevent drop-off|website=www.hulldailymail.co.uk|access-date=4 October 2023|archive-date=4 October 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231004073738/https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-fc-high-lows-mid-8799429|url-status=dead}}</ref>

===Doncaster RLFC===
On 20 November 2023, it was reported that he had signed for [[Doncaster RLFC]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on a two-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=20 Nov 2023|title=Ex-Hull FC star, Fiji international makes Championship move ahead of 2024: 'I can't wait'|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/ex-hull-fc-star-fiji-international-makes-championship-move-ahead-of-2024-i-cant-wait}}</ref>

===International===
He was selected to represent {{rlnt|Fiji}} in the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref>{{Cite news |url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/full-squads-for-every-team-at-the-2017-rugby-league-world-cup/news-story/161dcc489109c2a8bbf6a65746d5239e |title=Full squads for every team at the 2017 Rugby League World Cup |date=2017-10-17 |work=Daily Telegraph |access-date=2017-10-17 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171017100332/http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/full-squads-for-every-team-at-the-2017-rugby-league-world-cup/news-story/161dcc489109c2a8bbf6a65746d5239e |archive-date=2017-10-17}}</ref>
